q: Where are the values of a Dataverse choice column stored, and who can change them?
o: As table column metadata, editable only by an app maker
As ordinary rows any app user can edit
In a local collection on each screen
In the user's browser profile
why: Choice values live in column metadata and can only be modified by a maker, not end users.

q: You create a Choice column and leave 'Sync with global choice?' at its default. What is that default and its effect?
o: Yes, so the same choices can be reused in other columns and tables
No, so the choices stay local to this one column
Yes, but the list becomes read-only forever
No, and multiple selection is disabled
why: The default is Yes (global), letting the choice set be reused elsewhere; local (No) restricts it to that column/table.

q: Decide whether each statement about choice columns is true.

rows.s: A choice column can be configured to allow multiple selected values.
You should use global choices unless the values apply to a single column in a single table.
For a local choice, you must define each label and Power Apps assigns it an integer value.
End users can add new options to a choice list at run time.

why: Choices support single/multiple selection, integer-backed labels, and global reuse; only makers edit the list.

q: Before creating a choice column, what should most influence your decision to use a choice vs a lookup?
o: How the apps, automation and reporting will consume the data
The color you want each option to display
The number of screens in the app
Whether the app is tablet or phone layout
why: Choose choice vs lookup based on how the data will be used downstream by apps, flows and reports.

q: Why must you write Category = 'Category (Accounts)'.'Preferred Customer' rather than Category = 'Preferred Customer'?
o: The choice is an option-set/table, so you need the fully qualified table.column reference
'Preferred Customer' is a reserved word
Short names only work in galleries
Power Fx forbids spaces in values
why: A choice is itself an option set; comparing to a bare text gives an 'Incompatible types' error, so use the full reference.

q: A combo box lets users pick several categories. Which formula shows gallery rows matching ANY selected category?
o: Filter(Accounts, Category in ComboBoxCategory.SelectedItems)
Filter(Accounts, Category = ComboBoxCategory)
Search(Accounts, ComboBoxCategory, "Category")
Sort(Accounts, Category)
why: The in operator filters on membership across the multiple selected items.

q: To also show ALL rows when no category is selected, what do you add to Filter(Accounts, Category in ComboBoxCategory.SelectedItems ...)?
o: || IsEmpty(ComboBoxCategory.SelectedItems)
&& IsBlank(Accounts)
; ClearCollect(...)
a second nested ForAll
why: Adding || IsEmpty(...SelectedItems) returns all rows when the user has selected nothing.

q: Filtering on a multi-select 'choices' column raises a delegation warning. What is a recommended workaround?
o: Create a Dataverse view that filters the choices, then filter against that view
Increase the row limit to 2000 and ignore it
Store the choices in a global variable first
Convert the column to a text field
why: Because 'choices' is stored as a comma-separated list (non-delegable), a Dataverse view that pre-filters avoids the warning.

q: What does the Choices() function return when set as a combo box's Items for a lookup/choice column?
o: A table of the possible values built from the column's metadata
A single text string of the default value
The number of options available
A boolean indicating multi-select
why: Choices() prepares the list of possible values (a table) from the column metadata for selection.

q: Decide whether each statement about filtering choice columns is true.

rows.s: Forgetting the table prefix in a choice comparison yields an 'Incompatible types for comparison' error.
A Dataverse view workaround still lets the app user type the filter value at run time.
Choice and choices columns give consistent, standardized ways to filter rows.

why: The view approach avoids delegation but fixes the criteria (no runtime user input); missing the prefix errors out.
